structured.name: Change Availability Charging Station
structured.objective: To enable the CSMS to change the availability of a Charging Station.
structured.description:
This use case describes how the CSMS requests the Charging Station to change the availability.
A Charging Station is considered Operative when it is charging or ready for charging.
A Charging Station is considered Inoperative when it does not allow any charging.
scenario:
1. The CSMS sends a ChangeAvailabilityRequest for requesting a Charging Station to change its
availability.

2. Upon receipt of a ChangeAvailabilityRequest, the Charging Station responds with
ChangeAvailabilityResponse.
structured.postconditions:
Successful postcondition:
The CSMS was able to change the availability of the Charging Station.
When changing the availability of a Charging Station to Operative, the status of the Charging
Station has changed to Available.
When changing the availability of a Charging Station to Inoperative, the status of the Charging
Station has changed to Unavailable.
Failure postcondition:
The CSMS was not able to change the requested Charging Station’s availability.
structured.remarks:
Availabilty state set by ChangeAvailabilityRequest persists after a reboot. For example, if
Charging Station is set to Unavailable it remains unavailable after reboot.

requirements.precondition:
In the case the evse field is omitted in
ChangeAvailabilityRequest.
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station status change SHALL apply to
the whole Charging Station.

The Charging Station SHALL respond with
ChangeAvailabilityResponse.

This response message SHALL indicate whether
the Charging Station is able to change to the
requested availability.

requirements.precondition:
In the event that CSMS requests the
Charging Station to change to the
state it is already in.
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL respond with
availability status Accepted.

requirements.precondition:
When an availability change request
with ChangeAvailabilityRequest has
happened.
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL inform the CSMS by
sending the status of each of the changed
connectors via a NotifyEventRequest or a
StatusNotificationRequest.

requirements.precondition: When a transaction is in progress.
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL respond with
availability status Scheduled to indicate that it is
scheduled to occur after the transaction has
finished.

requirements.precondition:
When the availability of the Charging
Station becomes Inoperative
(Unavailable, Faulted)
requirements.definition:
All operative EVSEs and connectors (i.e. not
Faulted) SHALL become Unavailable.

requirements.precondition:
When the availability of the Charging
Station becomes Operative
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL revert the status of all
EVSEs and connectors to their original status.

requirements.precondition:
When the availability of a Charging
Station has been set explicitly via
ChangeAvailabilityRequest
requirements.definition:
The set availability state SHALL be persistent
across reboot/power loss.

sub_scenarios.objective: Reserve a specific EVSE at a Charging Station
steps: 1. EV Driver asks the CSMS to reserve a specific EVSE at the Charging Station.
2. The CSMS sends ReserveNowRequest with a EVSE to a Charging Station.

3. Upon receipt of ReserveNowRequest, the Charging Station responds with
ReserveNowResponse with status Accepted.

4. The Charging Station sends StatusNotificationRequest with the status Reserved for all
Connectors of that EVSE.
5. The CSMS responds with StatusNotificationResponse to the Charging Station.

sub_scenarios.objective: Reserve a connector type at a Charging Station
steps: 1. EV Driver asks the CSMS to reserve a connector type at the Charging Station.
2. The CSMS sends ReserveNowRequest with a connector type to a Charging Station.

3. Upon receipt of ReserveNowRequest, the Charging Station responds with
ReserveNowResponse with status Accepted.
sub_scenarios.prerequisites: The Charging Station has at least one available EVSE with the specified connector type
sub_scenarios.postconditions:
Successful postcondition:
The Charging Station has accepted the ReserveNowRequest
Failure postcondition:
The Charging Station has rejected the ReserveNowRequest

sub_scenarios.objective: Use an EVSE with connector status Reserved, that is reserved for this GroupIdToken
steps: 1. The EV Driver connects the cable.

2. Charging Station starts a transaction, but connector status remains
Reserved.

3. The EV Driver presents an IdTokenType at the Charging Station that is not the
same as the reservation’s IdTokenType, but the reservation contains a
groupIdToken.

4. Charging Station authorizes the IdTokenType via an AuthorizeRequest, Local
Authorization List or Authorization Cache, and checks if the groupIdToken of
the IdTokenType matches with the reservation.

5. If groupIdTokens match:
a. Connector status becomes Occupied, since reservation has now been
consumed.
b. Charging Station starts a transaction as in E02 - Start Transaction -
Cable Plugin First

chunks.content:
# G04 - Change Availability Charging Station

**Objective:** To enable the CSMS to change the availability of a Charging Station.

**Description:** This use case describes how the CSMS requests the Charging Station to change the availability.
A Charging Station is considered Operative when it is charging or ready for charging.
A Charging Station is considered Inoperative when it does not allow any charging.

**Actors:** Charging Station, CSMS

**Scenario:**
1. The CSMS sends a ChangeAvailabilityRequest for requesting a Charging Station to change its
availability.
2. Upon receipt of a ChangeAvailabilityRequest, the Charging Station responds with
ChangeAvailabilityResponse.

**Prerequisites:** n/a

**Postconditions:** Successful postcondition:
The CSMS was able to change the availability of the Charging Station.
When changing the availability of a Charging Station to Operative, the status of the Charging
Station has changed to Available.
When changing the availability of a Charging Station to Inoperative, the status of the Charging
Station has changed to Unavailable.
Failure postcondition:
The CSMS was not able to change the requested Charging Station’s availability.

**Remarks:** Availabilty state set by ChangeAvailabilityRequest persists after a reboot. For example, if
Charging Station is set to Unavailable it remains unavailable after reboot.

# G04 - Requirements

**G04.FR.01**
Precondition: In the case the evse field is omitted in
ChangeAvailabilityRequest.
The Charging Station status change SHALL apply to
the whole Charging Station.

**G04.FR.02**
Precondition: Upon receipt of
ChangeAvailabilityRequest.
The Charging Station SHALL respond with
ChangeAvailabilityResponse.

**G04.FR.03**
Precondition: G04.FR.02
This response message SHALL indicate whether
the Charging Station is able to change to the
requested availability.

**G04.FR.04**
Precondition: In the event that CSMS requests the
Charging Station to change to the
state it is already in.
The Charging Station SHALL respond with
availability status Accepted.

**G04.FR.05**
Precondition: When an availability change request
with ChangeAvailabilityRequest has
happened.
The Charging Station SHALL inform the CSMS by
sending the status of each of the changed
connectors via a NotifyEventRequest or a
StatusNotificationRequest.

**G04.FR.06**
Precondition: When a transaction is in progress.
The Charging Station SHALL respond with
availability status Scheduled to indicate that it is
scheduled to occur after the transaction has
finished.

**G04.FR.07**
Precondition: When the availability of the Charging
Station becomes Inoperative
(Unavailable, Faulted)
All operative EVSEs and connectors (i.e. not
Faulted) SHALL become Unavailable.

**G04.FR.08**
Precondition: When the availability of the Charging
Station becomes Operative
The Charging Station SHALL revert the status of all
EVSEs and connectors to their original status.

**G04.FR.09**
Precondition: When the availability of a Charging
Station has been set explicitly via
ChangeAvailabilityRequest
The set availability state SHALL be persistent
across reboot/power loss.
